summary: Published the crm-cleanup runx skill to GitHub and the hosted runx registry, opened PR #248 against runxhq/runx, and ran a dogfood test from the hosted registry that sealed successfully with a verified receipt. The skill correctly detected duplicates, missing fields, and stale entries in the test contacts without modifying any data, confirming read-only cleanup behavior. Harness passes 2/2 on the hosted registry.
observations: Skill published to GitHub at https://github.com/armstrongsam25/runx-crm-cleanup-skill (commit a62d996a67fbd7e9b3aa08f629f5eae957ca309b)

Skill available on hosted registry at https://runx.ai/x/armstrongsam25/crm-cleanup@sha-f8a57ff3d7ed with version sha-f8a57ff3d7ed and digest sha256:26c02f5d978e9abd082b60a5282a9d0b87eba2470e7ea9de355626b960cd3c9d
Harness status is passed with 2/2 cases (sealed_cleanup_report and stop_empty_contacts) verified on the hosted registry
PR #248 opened against runxhq/runx from armstrongsam25:skill/crm-cleanup to main, URL https://github.com/runxhq/runx/pull/248

Dogfood run against the hosted registry produced a sealed receipt (receipt_id sha256:1daf05565643a7fbed1bb5fcaadd52034437aeadf3730db7884f11689fa7a13c) with disposition closed and reason_code process_closed
Dogfood receipt verified via `runx verify` with valid:true, digest valid, content_address valid, and signature valid in production mode

Dogfood output correctly identified 1 duplicate set (c1 and c2 sharing email alice@example.com), 2 missing-field issues (both missing company), and 2 stale entries (c1 no_contact_date, c2 exceeds_threshold at 916 days)
The skill is read-only: no merge, delete, update, or move operations were executed; all actions in the report are advisory suggestions only
